title: Determine the exact value of R(B9, B9)
lean_gate_reason: PASS 1 indicates the exact theorem R(B9,B9)=38 is already covered in prior art, so Lean would formalize a rediscovery rather than seal a frontier publication packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Mathematically clean exact-value packet, but not a micro-paper candidate because the exact statement appears to be already known from prior art.
strongest_honest_claim: The local Goodman-plus-Paley argument correctly reproduces the known exact value R(B9,B9)=38, but this run does not establish novelty.
candidate_theorem_slice: A monochromatic-B9-free coloring of K37 lies on the Goodman equality boundary and has strongly regular parameters (37,18,8,9), realized by the Paley graph on 37 vertices; this is supporting structure for a known theorem, not a new frontier slice.
